questions.question: Which of the following distributions is typically used for small samples sizes?
questions.explanation: The Student's-t Distribution is used for small sample sizes, as it is an approximation of the normal distribution when the sample size is small.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the bootstrap method in statistics?
options: A) To calculate the standard deviation of a dataset
B) To estimate the population mean and standard deviation
C) To create a confidence interval
questions.explanation: The bootstrap method is used to create a confidence interval by resampling with replacement from the original dataset.

questions.question: Which distribution is characterized by having two heavy tails?
questions.explanation: A long-tailed distribution is characterized by having two heavy tails, as opposed to the normal distribution which has two light tails.

questions.question: What is the relationship between the Chi-square Distribution and the Poisson Distribution?
options: A) The Chi-square Distribution is a special case of the Poisson Distribution
B) The Poisson Distribution is a special case of the Chi-square Distribution
C) The Chi-square Distribution is an approximation of the Poisson Distribution
D) The Poisson Distribution is an approximation of the Chi-square Distribution
questions.explanation: The Chi-square Distribution is a special case of the Poisson Distribution, where the Poisson Distribution is used to model counts of events.

questions.question: Which test is used to compare two independent samples?
questions.explanation: The T-test is used to compare two independent samples, whereas the ANOVA test is used to compare three or more groups.
